{{commons category|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} [[JA:多治速比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430889}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = 多治速比売神社 |画像 = [[Image:Tajihayahime-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|堺市|Q193428}}{{jalink|南区 (堺市)|Q1041996|南区}}{{jalink|宮山台|Q60997456}}2丁3-1 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|7.4|N|135|29|52.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=多治速比売神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|比売神|Q22070227|多治速比売命}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = 伝・{{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}の時代（{{jalink|530年|Q30894}}頃） |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} |別名 = 荒山宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月5日に近い日曜日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=多治速
比売神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:多治速比売神社拝殿 堺市南区にて Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|拝殿]] [[File:多治速比売神社本殿と鴨田社 堺市南区にて 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|本殿（重要文化財）]] '''多治速比売神社'''（たじはやひめじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|堺市|Q193428}}{{jalink|南区 (堺市)|Q1041996|南区}}{{jalink|宮山台|Q60997456}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に記載されている{{jalink|和泉国|Q1047117}}{{jalink|大鳥郡|Q3270651}}の{{jalink|式内社}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。'''荒山宮'''（こうぜんのみや）とも呼ばれ、境内の周りは{{jalink|荒山公園|Q11618489}}として賑わっている。 === 歴史 === {{jalink|和泉国|Q1047117}}{{jalink|大鳥郡|Q3270651}}の延喜式内社二十四座のひとつで{{jalink|宣化天皇|Q329728}}の時代（{{jalink|530年|Q30894}}頃）の創建と伝えられている。{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}初年までは総福寺と並存した{{jalink|神宮寺|Q1074971}}であったが、{{jalink|神仏分離|Q1970535}}の際神社のみとなった。後に{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列せられている。本殿は{{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}の建造物で国の{{jalink|重要文化財|Q1188622}}に指定されている。境内には13の末社「坂上神社（式内社）、鴨田神社（式内社）、大神社、住吉社、天照社、八幡社、春日社、熊野社、白山社、弁天社、稲荷社、福石社、水天宮」があり、合わせて荒山宮と呼ばれている。 {{jalink|1963年|Q2755}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}38年）、{{jalink|泉北ニュータウン|Q7450095}}の造成計画に伴い、社有地の大部分が売却され、住宅や{{jalink|荒山公園|Q11618489}}が作られた。それにより、当社は境内の整備や拝殿・参集殿等の大改築が行われている。 === 祭神 === * 主祭神 - {{jalink|比売神|Q22070227|多治速比売命}}（たじはやひめのみこと） * 合祀神 - {{jalink|スサノオ|Q272993|素盞嗚尊}}、{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}公 === 境内 === * 本殿（{{jalink|重要文化財|Q1188622}}） - {{jalink|室町時代|Q334845}}の{{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}8年（{{jalink|1539年|Q6479}}）から天文12年（{{jalink|1543年|Q6523}}）の間の再建。様式は三間社入母屋造で正面に千鳥破風があり大きな向拝があって、それに軒唐破風がついている。向拝柱上部中央の{{jalink|蟇股|Q123117911}}には龍・雲・浪、左右の蟇股には表面に牡丹・唐獅子、裏面に雲・宝珠、内法長押正面中央の蟇股には桐、右の蟇股には鯱、左の蟇股には山茶花・松・幣が、北と南の側面蟇股には鯉・松・滝・雲がそれぞれ彫られている。向拝の{{jalink|手挟|Q124660898}}は{{jalink|透彫|Q17055244}}で左右二個あり、向かって右は右面に芭蕉に蟷螂、左面に水に蓮、向かって左は右面に海藻と貝類、左面に水に花菖蒲が彫られている。向拝の木鼻は獣頭のような変わった珍しい形が付けてある。芭蕉に蟷螂の彫刻は全国でもここだけとされる。解体修理の際、天文8年（1539年）と10年（{{jalink|1541年|Q6516}}）の墨書が発見され、{{jalink|文政|Q1327119}}2年（{{jalink|1819年|Q6967}}）の棟札の写しには、天文12年（1543年）2月に再建されたと書かれていたことから天文8年から12年にわたって建てられたことが明らかとなった。 * 鴨田神社 - 祭神：不詳。本殿に向かって右手にある{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|1908年|Q2056}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}41年）に{{jalink|泉北郡|Q646275}}{{jalink|北上神村|Q11400869}}大字大平寺字鴨田から合祀。{{jalink|賀茂別雷命|Q11634943|加茂別雷命}}、{{jalink|住吉三神|Q1114013|住吉命}}、{{jalink|事代主命}}、{{jalink|武内宿禰|Q1073668|武内宿禰命}}、{{jalink|大田田根子|Q55536798}}、{{jalink|大己貴命}}などとされるがいずれも根拠は不詳。 * 坂上神社 - 祭神：不詳。本殿に向かって左手にある式内社。{{jalink|1910年|Q2075}}（明治43年）に泉北郡{{jalink|久世村 (大阪府)|Q11368908|久世村}}大字平井字南垣外から合祀。『{{jalink|姓氏録}}』和泉国諸藩によると{{jalink|池辺直}}は{{jalink|坂上氏|Q10928477|坂上大宿禰}}と同祖で阿智王の後裔であるという。また『和泉国神名帳』{{jalink|大鳥郡|Q3270651}}の項では日下部大歳社と神代社、坂上社などの中間に記載されている従四位上の神階をもつ池辺檜原社・池辺神殿社・池辺社の三社は記載順から蜂田郷か日下部郷にあった神社と考えられている。これらの事から蜂田郷の平井に所在する坂上神社は池辺氏もしくは坂上氏が祖先の{{jalink|阿知使主|Q11657596}}（阿智王）を祀ったのではないかと推測されている。『堺市史 続編』第一巻（第一篇 古代 p179）は古代において池辺直一族が大鳥郡内に蟠踞した可能性が大きいとしている。 * 葛神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|一言主|Q10864079|一言主神}}。久世村大字平井字中垣から合祀。 * 金峯神社 - 祭神：{{jalink|金山彦神|Q4387856|金山比古命}}・金山比売命。久世村大字平井字南垣外から合祀。 * 天照社 * 大神社 * 住吉社 * 拝殿 * 白山社 * 熊野社 * 春日社 * 弁天社（厳島神社） - 祭神：{{jalink|イチキシマヒメ|Q11059454|市杵嶋姫命}}。{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}（明治40年）に泉北郡北上神村大字大庭寺字厳島から合祀。 * {{jalink|伊勢神宮|Q687168}}遥拝所 * 吉田民藏宮司の像 * 金高稲荷社 - 祭神：{{jalink|稲荷神|Q719665|稲荷魂命}} * 脇門 * 福石社 - 祭神：福石大神 * 旧大師堂 * 八幡社 - 祭神：{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637|誉田別命}}。{{jalink|1909年|Q2057}}（明治42年）に泉北郡久世村大字伏尾字伏尾山から、更に1910年（明治43年）に泉北郡久世村大字和田字山の上から合祀。 * 社務所 * 儀式殿 * 参集殿 * 神館 * 水天宮 === 文化財 === === 重要文化財 === * 本殿 附：棟札（1枚） === 祭事 === * 歳旦祭 ** {{jalink|1月1日|Q2150}} 午前8時 * 古武道奉納 ** {{jalink|1月3日|Q2156}} 午前10時 * 福石社神事 ** {{jalink|1月6日|Q2221}} 午後2時 * とんど焼き ** 1月第3日曜日 午前9時～午後４時まで * 節分祭 ** {{jalink|2月3日|Q2315}} 午前10時 * 厄除火焚神事 ** 2月3日 午後7時～ * 稲荷祭 ** 3月最終の日曜日 午前10時 * 夏越の大祓式 ** {{jalink|6月30日|Q2657}} 午後4時 * 弁天祭 ** 8月最終の日曜日 午後6時 * 例大祭 ** {{jalink|10月5日|Q2930}}に近い日曜日 午前9時 ** 氏子による{{jalink|だんじり|Q11271630}}や神輿だんじりの宮入等で賑わう * 七五三詣 ** {{jalink|11月15日|Q2982}} * 年越の大祓式 ** {{jalink|12月31日|Q2912}} * 月次祭 ** 毎月1日 午前6時 === 現地情報 === * {{jalink|南海泉北線|Q2758012}} {{jalink|泉ヶ丘駅}}から{{jalink|南海バス|Q11408154}}｢宮山台回り」か「堺東行き」または「（泉北2号線経由）津久野駅行き」に乗り、「宮山台2丁」下車。 === 脚注 === === 参考文献 === * {{jalink|大阪府教育委員会|Q7105593}} 編『重要文化財多治速比売神社本殿修理報告書』1956年 * 大阪府の歴史散歩編集委員会『新版 大阪府の歴史散歩 下』山川出版社、1990年、181頁 * 『堺・泉州』第13号 堺泉州出版会、2002年、79-81頁 * 『堺歴史読本』KADOKAWA、2015年、117頁 === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|蔀関月|Q11621791}} - {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}中後期の{{jalink|大坂}}で活動した{{jalink|浮世絵師|Q33082999}}。平井の坂上神社から{{jalink|三十六歌仙|Q671039}}扁額が{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}43年（{{jalink|1910年|Q2075}}）に移された。元は36面あったはずだが、現在は半分のみ残る。この内{{jalink|中務|Q460152}}の裏面墨書に「畫者　法橋関月蔀氏」とあり、これ以前に法橋位を得ていたことがわかる。 * {{jalink|鈴木貫太郎|Q335198}} - 第42代{{jalink|内閣総理大臣|Q274948}}。幼年に生地を去っているが、多治速比売神社への崇敬の念が深く中佐時代にも参拝した。海軍中将鈴木貫太郎名で「敬神崇祖」と書かれた大きな扁額が残されており、現在は氏子参集殿に掲げられており、同文の扇面が複製され氏子に分けられた。大正12年の境内摂社稲荷社の本殿改築及玉垣新築に際して、海軍中将鈴木貫太郎の名で奉納した玉垣石が残されている。また{{jalink|二・二六事件|Q477958}}で重症を負った時、多治速比売命が枕元にお立ちになり命を救われたとして、翌年一月にたか夫人と本復祝の参拝をしている。戦後、鈴木神社創建の話が出た際は多治速比売神社の境内が適していると相談があったという。 === 外部リンク === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ 多治速比売神社トップページ]（公式サイト） {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たしはやめしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}} {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}. It is the {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} of {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, which is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}. The old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}}. === History === {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}}{{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}). Until the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{jalink|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. The main shrine is a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} building and is designated as a national {{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}38), with the development plan of {{jalink|Senboku New Town|Q7450095}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{jalink|Himekami|Q22070227|Tajhayahime no Mikoto}} * Combined deities - {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Sujinson}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{jalink|Important cultural property|Q1188622}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{jalink|1539|Q6479}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{jalink|1543|Q6523}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{jalink|toad-mata|Q123117911}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{jalink|hand clip|Q124660898}} of the facing prayer is {{jalink|openwork|Q17055244}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{jalink|1541|Q6516}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1908|Q2056}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}41) from {{jalink|Senboku District|Q646275}}{{jalink|Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshirosu no Mikoto}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Taneko Ota|Q55536798}}, {{jalink|Omi Takamikoto}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{jalink|1910|Q2075}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{jalink|Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|Kuse Village}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{jalink|surname record}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{jalink|Nao Ikebe}} is the same ancestor as {{jalink|Sakagami clan|Q10928477|Sakagami Ōsukune}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{jalink|Achi Messhi|Q11657596}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|Hitototo Toto no Kami}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko no Mikoto}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari Spirit Life}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Hachiman God|Q261637|Honda Bessumei}}. {{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Sanshuden * Shinkan * Suitengu Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property === * Main shrine attached: ridge plate (1 piece) === Festival === * Saidan Festival ** {{jalink|January 1st|Q2150}} 8am * Kobudo dedication ** {{jalink|January 3rd|Q2156}} 10am * Fukuishi Shrine ritual ** {{jalink|January 6th|Q2221}} 2pm * Tondoyaki ** Third Sunday in January from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. * Setsubun Festival ** {{jalink|February 3rd|Q2315}} 10am * Fire-burning ritual to ward off evil spirits **February 3rd from 7pm * Inari Festival ** Last Sunday in March 10am * Summer exorcism ceremony ** {{jalink|June 30th|Q2657}} 4pm * Benten Festival **Last Sunday in August 6pm * Reitaisai ** Sunday 9am near {{jalink|October 5th|Q2930}} ** Crowded with parishioners holding {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} and mikoshi danjiri shrine entrances. * Shichigosan pilgrimage ** {{jalink|November 15th|Q2982}} * New year's purification ceremony ** {{jalink|December 31st|Q2912}} * Monthly festival ** 6am on the 1st of every month === Local information === * {{jalink|Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012}} From {{jalink|Izumigaoka Station}}, take the {{jalink|Nankai Bus|Q11408154}} "Miyayamadai-bound", "Sakai-Higashi bound", or "(via Senboku Route 2) bound for Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593}} “Important Cultural Property Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall Repair Report” 1956 * Osaka Prefecture History Walk Editorial Committee, New Edition Osaka Prefecture History Walk Part 2, Yamakawa Publishing, 1990, p. 181 * "Sakai/Senshu" No. 13, Sakai Senshu Publishing, 2002, pp. 79-81 * “Sakai History Reader” KADOKAWA, 2015, p. 117 === Related items === * {{jalink|Yu Guangetsu|Q11621791}} - {{jalink|Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999}} who was active in {{jalink|Osaka}} in the middle and late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. The {{jalink|Thirty-six Poems|Q671039}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}43 ({{jalink|1910|Q2075}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{jalink|Nakagaku|Q460152}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{jalink|Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198}} - 42nd {{jalink|Prime Minister|Q274948}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{jalink|February 26 Incident|Q477958}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashihayame Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} [[JA:多治速比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430889}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tajisuhime Shrine |Image = [[Image:Tajihayahime-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}}{{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}2-3-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|30|7.4|N|135|29|52.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tajisuhime Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Hime-kami|Q22070227|Tajiso Hime-no-Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend/Era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |Alternative name = Arayama Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = Sunday closest to October 5th |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tajiso
Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}} {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}. It is the {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} of {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, which is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}. The old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}}. === History === {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}}{{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}). Until the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{jalink|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. The main shrine is a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} building and is designated as a national {{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}38), with the development plan of {{jalink|Senboku New Town|Q7450095}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{jalink|Himekami|Q22070227|Tajhayahime no Mikoto}} * Combined deities - {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Sujinson}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{jalink|Important cultural property|Q1188622}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{jalink|1539|Q6479}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{jalink|1543|Q6523}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{jalink|toad-mata|Q123117911}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{jalink|hand clip|Q124660898}} of the facing prayer is {{jalink|openwork|Q17055244}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{jalink|1541|Q6516}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1908|Q2056}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}41) from {{jalink|Senboku District|Q646275}}{{jalink|Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshirosu no Mikoto}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Taneko Ota|Q55536798}}, {{jalink|Omi Takamikoto}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{jalink|1910|Q2075}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{jalink|Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|Kuse Village}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{jalink|surname record}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{jalink|Nao Ikebe}} is the same ancestor as {{jalink|Sakagami clan|Q10928477|Sakagami Ōsukune}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{jalink|Achi Messhi|Q11657596}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|Hitototo Toto no Kami}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko no Mikoto}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari Spirit Life}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Hachiman God|Q261637|Honda Bessumei}}. {{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Sanshuden * Shinkan * Suitengu Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property === * Main shrine attached: ridge plate (1 piece) === Festival === * Saidan Festival ** {{jalink|January 1st|Q2150}} 8am * Kobudo dedication ** {{jalink|January 3rd|Q2156}} 10am * Fukuishi Shrine ritual ** {{jalink|January 6th|Q2221}} 2pm * Tondoyaki ** Third Sunday in January from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. * Setsubun Festival ** {{jalink|February 3rd|Q2315}} 10am * Fire-burning ritual to ward off evil spirits **February 3rd from 7pm * Inari Festival ** Last Sunday in March 10am * Summer exorcism ceremony ** {{jalink|June 30th|Q2657}} 4pm * Benten Festival **Last Sunday in August 6pm * Reitaisai ** Sunday 9am near {{jalink|October 5th|Q2930}} ** Crowded with parishioners holding {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} and mikoshi danjiri shrine entrances. * Shichigosan pilgrimage ** {{jalink|November 15th|Q2982}} * New year's purification ceremony ** {{jalink|December 31st|Q2912}} * Monthly festival ** 6am on the 1st of every month === Local information === * {{jalink|Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012}} From {{jalink|Izumigaoka Station}}, take the {{jalink|Nankai Bus|Q11408154}} "Miyayamadai-bound", "Sakai-Higashi bound", or "(via Senboku Route 2) bound for Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593}} “Important Cultural Property Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall Repair Report” 1956 * Osaka Prefecture History Walk Editorial Committee, New Edition Osaka Prefecture History Walk Part 2, Yamakawa Publishing, 1990, p. 181 * "Sakai/Senshu" No. 13, Sakai Senshu Publishing, 2002, pp. 79-81 * “Sakai History Reader” KADOKAWA, 2015, p. 117 === Related items === * {{jalink|Yu Guangetsu|Q11621791}} - {{jalink|Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999}} who was active in {{jalink|Osaka}} in the middle and late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. The {{jalink|Thirty-six Poems|Q671039}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}43 ({{jalink|1910|Q2075}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{jalink|Nakagaku|Q460152}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{jalink|Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198}} - 42nd {{jalink|Prime Minister|Q274948}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{jalink|February 26 Incident|Q477958}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashihayame Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} [[JA:多治速比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430889}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tajisuhime Shrine |Image = [[Image:Tajihayahime-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}}{{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}2-3-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|30|7.4|N|135|29|52.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tajisuhime Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Hime-kami|Q22070227|Tajiso Hime-no-Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend/Era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |Alternative name = Arayama Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = Sunday closest to October 5th |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tajiso
Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}} {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}. It is the {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} of {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, which is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}. The old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}}. === History === {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}}{{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}). Until the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{jalink|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. The main shrine is a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} building and is designated as a national {{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}38), with the development plan of {{jalink|Senboku New Town|Q7450095}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{jalink|Himekami|Q22070227|Tajhayahime no Mikoto}} * Combined deities - {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Sujinson}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{jalink|Important cultural property|Q1188622}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{jalink|1539|Q6479}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{jalink|1543|Q6523}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{jalink|toad-mata|Q123117911}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{jalink|hand clip|Q124660898}} of the facing prayer is {{jalink|openwork|Q17055244}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{jalink|1541|Q6516}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1908|Q2056}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}41) from {{jalink|Senboku District|Q646275}}{{jalink|Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshirosu no Mikoto}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Taneko Ota|Q55536798}}, {{jalink|Omi Takamikoto}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{jalink|1910|Q2075}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{jalink|Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|Kuse Village}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{jalink|surname record}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{jalink|Nao Ikebe}} is the same ancestor as {{jalink|Sakagami clan|Q10928477|Sakagami Ōsukune}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{jalink|Achi Messhi|Q11657596}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|Hitototo Toto no Kami}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko no Mikoto}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari Spirit Life}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Hachiman God|Q261637|Honda Bessumei}}. {{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Sanshuden * Shinkan * Suitengu Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property === * Main shrine attached: ridge plate (1 piece) === Festival === * Saidan Festival ** {{jalink|January 1st|Q2150}} 8am * Kobudo dedication ** {{jalink|January 3rd|Q2156}} 10am * Fukuishi Shrine ritual ** {{jalink|January 6th|Q2221}} 2pm * Tondoyaki ** Third Sunday in January from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. * Setsubun Festival ** {{jalink|February 3rd|Q2315}} 10am * Fire-burning ritual to ward off evil spirits **February 3rd from 7pm * Inari Festival ** Last Sunday in March 10am * Summer exorcism ceremony ** {{jalink|June 30th|Q2657}} 4pm * Benten Festival **Last Sunday in August 6pm * Reitaisai ** Sunday 9am near {{jalink|October 5th|Q2930}} ** Crowded with parishioners holding {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} and mikoshi danjiri shrine entrances. * Shichigosan pilgrimage ** {{jalink|November 15th|Q2982}} * New year's purification ceremony ** {{jalink|December 31st|Q2912}} * Monthly festival ** 6am on the 1st of every month === Local information === * {{jalink|Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012}} From {{jalink|Izumigaoka Station}}, take the {{jalink|Nankai Bus|Q11408154}} "Miyayamadai-bound", "Sakai-Higashi bound", or "(via Senboku Route 2) bound for Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593}} “Important Cultural Property Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall Repair Report” 1956 * Osaka Prefecture History Walk Editorial Committee, New Edition Osaka Prefecture History Walk Part 2, Yamakawa Publishing, 1990, p. 181 * "Sakai/Senshu" No. 13, Sakai Senshu Publishing, 2002, pp. 79-81 * “Sakai History Reader” KADOKAWA, 2015, p. 117 === Related items === * {{jalink|Yu Guangetsu|Q11621791}} - {{jalink|Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999}} who was active in {{jalink|Osaka}} in the middle and late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. The {{jalink|Thirty-six Poems|Q671039}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}43 ({{jalink|1910|Q2075}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{jalink|Nakagaku|Q460152}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{jalink|Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198}} - 42nd {{jalink|Prime Minister|Q274948}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{jalink|February 26 Incident|Q477958}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashihayame Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{commons category|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} [[JA:多治速比売神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11430889}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Tajisuhime Shrine |Image = [[Image:Tajihayahime-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Torii and worship hall |Location = {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}}{{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}2-3-1 |Position={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|30|7.4|N|135|29|52.4|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tajisuhime Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Hime-kami|Q22070227|Tajiso Hime-no-Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company rank = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} (small)
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend/Era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |Alternative name = Arayama Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = Sunday closest to October 5th |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tajiso
Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}} {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}. It is the {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} of {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, which is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}. The old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}}. === History === {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}}{{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}). Until the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{jalink|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. The main shrine is a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} building and is designated as a national {{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}38), with the development plan of {{jalink|Senboku New Town|Q7450095}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{jalink|Himekami|Q22070227|Tajhayahime no Mikoto}} * Combined deities - {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Sujinson}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{jalink|Important cultural property|Q1188622}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{jalink|1539|Q6479}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{jalink|1543|Q6523}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{jalink|toad-mata|Q123117911}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{jalink|hand clip|Q124660898}} of the facing prayer is {{jalink|openwork|Q17055244}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{jalink|1541|Q6516}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1908|Q2056}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}41) from {{jalink|Senboku District|Q646275}}{{jalink|Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshirosu no Mikoto}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Taneko Ota|Q55536798}}, {{jalink|Omi Takamikoto}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{jalink|1910|Q2075}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{jalink|Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|Kuse Village}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{jalink|surname record}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{jalink|Nao Ikebe}} is the same ancestor as {{jalink|Sakagami clan|Q10928477|Sakagami Ōsukune}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{jalink|Achi Messhi|Q11657596}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|Hitototo Toto no Kami}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko no Mikoto}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari Spirit Life}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Hachiman God|Q261637|Honda Bessumei}}. {{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Sanshuden * Shinkan * Suitengu Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property === * Main shrine attached: ridge plate (1 piece) === Festival === * Saidan Festival ** {{jalink|January 1st|Q2150}} 8am * Kobudo dedication ** {{jalink|January 3rd|Q2156}} 10am * Fukuishi Shrine ritual ** {{jalink|January 6th|Q2221}} 2pm * Tondoyaki ** Third Sunday in January from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. * Setsubun Festival ** {{jalink|February 3rd|Q2315}} 10am * Fire-burning ritual to ward off evil spirits **February 3rd from 7pm * Inari Festival ** Last Sunday in March 10am * Summer exorcism ceremony ** {{jalink|June 30th|Q2657}} 4pm * Benten Festival **Last Sunday in August 6pm * Reitaisai ** Sunday 9am near {{jalink|October 5th|Q2930}} ** Crowded with parishioners holding {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} and mikoshi danjiri shrine entrances. * Shichigosan pilgrimage ** {{jalink|November 15th|Q2982}} * New year's purification ceremony ** {{jalink|December 31st|Q2912}} * Monthly festival ** 6am on the 1st of every month === Local information === * {{jalink|Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012}} From {{jalink|Izumigaoka Station}}, take the {{jalink|Nankai Bus|Q11408154}} "Miyayamadai-bound", "Sakai-Higashi bound", or "(via Senboku Route 2) bound for Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593}} “Important Cultural Property Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall Repair Report” 1956 * Osaka Prefecture History Walk Editorial Committee, New Edition Osaka Prefecture History Walk Part 2, Yamakawa Publishing, 1990, p. 181 * "Sakai/Senshu" No. 13, Sakai Senshu Publishing, 2002, pp. 79-81 * “Sakai History Reader” KADOKAWA, 2015, p. 117 === Related items === * {{jalink|Yu Guangetsu|Q11621791}} - {{jalink|Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999}} who was active in {{jalink|Osaka}} in the middle and late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. The {{jalink|Thirty-six Poems|Q671039}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}43 ({{jalink|1910|Q2075}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{jalink|Nakagaku|Q460152}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{jalink|Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198}} - 42nd {{jalink|Prime Minister|Q274948}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{jalink|February 26 Incident|Q477958}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashihayame Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Sakai City|Q193428}}{{jalink|Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|Minami Ward}} {{jalink|Shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Miyayamadai|Q60997456}}. It is the {{jalink|Shikiuchi Shrine}} of {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, which is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932}}. The old {{jalink|shakaku|Q10444029}} is {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}}. === History === {{jalink|Izumi Province|Q1047117}}{{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{jalink|Emperor Senka|Q329728}} (around {{jalink|530|Q30894}}). Until the first year of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}, it was {{jalink|Jinguji|Q1074971}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{jalink|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{jalink|gosha|Q134917316}}. The main shrine is a {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} building and is designated as a national {{jalink|Important Cultural Property|Q1188622}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{jalink|1963|Q2755}} ({{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}38), with the development plan of {{jalink|Senboku New Town|Q7450095}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{jalink|Arayama Park|Q11618489}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{jalink|Himekami|Q22070227|Tajhayahime no Mikoto}} * Combined deities - {{jalink|Susanoo|Q272993|Sujinson}}, {{jalink|Michizane Sugawara|Q382005}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{jalink|Important cultural property|Q1188622}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{jalink|1539|Q6479}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{jalink|1543|Q6523}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{jalink|toad-mata|Q123117911}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{jalink|hand clip|Q124660898}} of the facing prayer is {{jalink|openwork|Q17055244}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{jalink|1541|Q6516}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1908|Q2056}} ({{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}41) from {{jalink|Senboku District|Q646275}}{{jalink|Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{jalink|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|Sumiyoshi no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Kotoshirosu no Mikoto}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Taneko Ota|Q55536798}}, {{jalink|Omi Takamikoto}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{jalink|1910|Q2075}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{jalink|Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|Kuse Village}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{jalink|surname record}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{jalink|Nao Ikebe}} is the same ancestor as {{jalink|Sakagami clan|Q10928477|Sakagami Ōsukune}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{jalink|Otori County|Q3270651}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{jalink|Achi Messhi|Q11657596}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|Hitototo Toto no Kami}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|Kanayamahiko no Mikoto}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{jalink|Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}. It was enshrined in {{jalink|1907|Q2048}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{jalink|Ise Jingu|Q687168}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Inari God|Q719665|Inari Spirit Life}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{jalink|Hachiman God|Q261637|Honda Bessumei}}. {{jalink|1909|Q2057}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Sanshuden * Shinkan * Suitengu Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property === * Main shrine attached: ridge plate (1 piece) === Festival === * Saidan Festival ** {{jalink|January 1st|Q2150}} 8am * Kobudo dedication ** {{jalink|January 3rd|Q2156}} 10am * Fukuishi Shrine ritual ** {{jalink|January 6th|Q2221}} 2pm * Tondoyaki ** Third Sunday in January from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. * Setsubun Festival ** {{jalink|February 3rd|Q2315}} 10am * Fire-burning ritual to ward off evil spirits **February 3rd from 7pm * Inari Festival ** Last Sunday in March 10am * Summer exorcism ceremony ** {{jalink|June 30th|Q2657}} 4pm * Benten Festival **Last Sunday in August 6pm * Reitaisai ** Sunday 9am near {{jalink|October 5th|Q2930}} ** Crowded with parishioners holding {{jalink|danjiri|Q11271630}} and mikoshi danjiri shrine entrances. * Shichigosan pilgrimage ** {{jalink|November 15th|Q2982}} * New year's purification ceremony ** {{jalink|December 31st|Q2912}} * Monthly festival ** 6am on the 1st of every month === Local information === * {{jalink|Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012}} From {{jalink|Izumigaoka Station}}, take the {{jalink|Nankai Bus|Q11408154}} "Miyayamadai-bound", "Sakai-Higashi bound", or "(via Senboku Route 2) bound for Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{jalink|Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593}} “Important Cultural Property Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall Repair Report” 1956 * Osaka Prefecture History Walk Editorial Committee, New Edition Osaka Prefecture History Walk Part 2, Yamakawa Publishing, 1990, p. 181 * "Sakai/Senshu" No. 13, Sakai Senshu Publishing, 2002, pp. 79-81 * “Sakai History Reader” KADOKAWA, 2015, p. 117 === Related items === * {{jalink|Yu Guangetsu|Q11621791}} - {{jalink|Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999}} who was active in {{jalink|Osaka}} in the middle and late {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}. The {{jalink|Thirty-six Poems|Q671039}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}43 ({{jalink|1910|Q2075}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{jalink|Nakagaku|Q460152}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{jalink|Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198}} - 42nd {{jalink|Prime Minister|Q274948}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{jalink|February 26 Incident|Q477958}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Tashihayame Shinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:和泉国の式内社]] [[Category:堺市の神社]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:堺市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:室町時代の建築]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = Legend/Era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} (around {{ill|lt=530|Q30894|WD=Q30894}}) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |Alternative name = Arayama Shrine |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular festival = Sunday closest to October 5th |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tajiso
Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q193428|WD=Q193428}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward}} {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyayamadai|Q60997456|WD=Q60997456}}. It is the {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|UNKNOWN}} of {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651}}, which is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}. The old {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} is {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117}}{{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728}} (around {{ill|lt=530|Q30894|WD=Q30894}}). Until the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}, it was {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. The main shrine is a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} building and is designated as a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}38), with the development plan of {{ill|lt=Senboku New Town|Q7450095|WD=Q7450095}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{ill|lt=Himekami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Tajhayahime no Mikoto}} * Combined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Sujinson}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{ill|lt=Important cultural property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{ill|lt=1539|Q6479|WD=Q6479}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{ill|lt=1543|Q6523|WD=Q6523}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{ill|lt=toad-mata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{ill|lt=hand clip|Q124660898|WD=Q124660898}} of the facing prayer is {{ill|lt=openwork|Q17055244|WD=Q17055244}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{ill|lt=1541|Q6516|WD=Q6516}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}41) from {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q646275|WD=Q646275}}{{ill|lt=Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869|WD=Q11400869}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi no Mikoto}}, {{ill||lt=Kotoshirosu no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Taneko Ota|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798}}, {{ill||lt=Omi Takamikoto|UNKNOWN}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{ill|lt=Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|WD=Q11368908|lt=Kuse Village}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{ill||lt=surname record|UNKNOWN}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{ill||lt=Nao Ikebe|UNKNOWN}} is the same ancestor as {{ill|lt=Sakagami clan|Q10928477|WD=Q10928477|lt=Sakagami Ōsukune}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{ill|lt=Achi Messhi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitototo Toto no Kami}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko no Mikoto}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto}}. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Inari God|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Spirit Life}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman God|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Honda Bessumei}}. {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Sanshuden * Shinkan * Suitengu {{ill||lt=Izumigaoka Station|UNKNOWN}}, take the {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus|Q11408154|WD=Q11408154}} "Miyayamadai-bound", "Sakai-Higashi bound", or "(via Senboku Route 2) bound for Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593|WD=Q7105593}} “Important Cultural Property Tajisuhime Shrine Main
