It is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, which is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The old {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} is {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} (around {{ill|lt=530|Q30894|WD=Q30894|1=530}}). Until the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The main shrine is a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} building and is designated as a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}38), with the development plan of {{ill|lt=Senboku New Town|Q7450095|WD=Q7450095|1=Senboku New Town}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{ill|lt=Himekami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Tajhayahime no Mikoto|1=Himegami}} * Combined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Sujinson|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{ill|lt=Important cultural property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{ill|lt=1539|Q6479|WD=Q6479|1=1539}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{ill|lt=1543|Q6523|WD=Q6523|1=1543}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{ill|lt=toad-mata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{ill|lt=hand clip|Q124660898|WD=Q124660898|1=tabasami}} of the facing prayer is {{ill|lt=openwork|Q17055244|WD=Q17055244|1=Sukashibori}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{ill|lt=1541|Q6516|WD=Q6516|1=1541}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}41) from {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q646275|WD=Q646275|1=Senboku District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869|WD=Q11400869|1=Kitaniwa}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshirosu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Taneko Ota|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|1=Ōtataneko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Omi Takamikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{ill|lt=Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|WD=Q11368908|lt=Kuse Village|1=Kuze}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=surname record|1=UNKNOWN}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nao Ikebe|1=UNKNOWN}} is the same ancestor as {{ill|lt=Sakagami clan|Q10928477|WD=Q10928477|lt=Sakagami Ōsukune|1=Sakanoue clan}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{ill|lt=Achi Messhi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitototo Toto no Kami|1=Hitokotonushi}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko no Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Inari God|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Spirit Life|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman God|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Honda Bessumei|1=Hachiman}}. {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593|WD=Q7105593|1=Osaka Prefectural Board of Publishing, 1990, p. 181 * "Sakai/Senshu" No. 13, Sakai Senshu Publishing, 2002, pp. 79-81 * “Sakai History Reader” KADOKAWA, 2015, p. 117 === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Yu Guangetsu|Q11621791|WD=Q11621791|1=Shitomi Kangetsu}} - {{ill|lt=Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999|WD=Q33082999|1=ukiyo-e artist}} who was active in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka|1=UNKNOWN}} in the middle and late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. The {{ill|lt=Thirty-six Poems|Q671039|WD=Q671039|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}43 ({{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{ill|lt=Nakagaku|Q460152|WD=Q460152|1=Nakatsukasa}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{ill|lt=Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198|WD=Q335198|1=Kantarō Suzuki}} - 42nd {{ill|lt=Prime Minister|Q274948|WD=Q274948|1=Prime Minister of Japan}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{ill|lt=February 26 Incident|Q477958|WD=Q477958|1=February 26 incident}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyayamadai|Q60997456|WD=Q60997456|1=Miyayamadai}}. It is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, which is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The old {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} is {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} (around {{ill|lt=530|Q30894|WD=Q30894|1=530}}). Until the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The main shrine is a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} building and is designated as a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}38), with the development plan of {{ill|lt=Senboku New Town|Q7450095|WD=Q7450095|1=Senboku New Town}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{ill|lt=Himekami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Tajhayahime no Mikoto|1=Himegami}} * Combined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Sujinson|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{ill|lt=Important cultural property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{ill|lt=1539|Q6479|WD=Q6479|1=1539}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{ill|lt=1543|Q6523|WD=Q6523|1=1543}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{ill|lt=toad-mata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{ill|lt=hand clip|Q124660898|WD=Q124660898|1=tabasami}} of the facing prayer is {{ill|lt=openwork|Q17055244|WD=Q17055244|1=Sukashibori}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{ill|lt=1541|Q6516|WD=Q6516|1=1541}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}41) from {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q646275|WD=Q646275|1=Senboku District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869|WD=Q11400869|1=Kitaniwa}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshirosu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Taneko Ota|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|1=Ōtataneko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Omi Takamikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{ill|lt=Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|WD=Q11368908|lt=Kuse Village|1=Kuze}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=surname record|1=UNKNOWN}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nao Ikebe|1=UNKNOWN}} is the same ancestor as {{ill|lt=Sakagami clan|Q10928477|WD=Q10928477|lt=Sakagami Ōsukune|1=Sakanoue clan}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{ill|lt=Achi Messhi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitototo Toto no Kami|1=Hitokotonushi}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko no Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Inari God|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Spirit Life|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman God|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Honda Bessumei|1=Hachiman}}. {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Guangetsu|Q11621791|WD=Q11621791|1=Shitomi Kangetsu}} - {{ill|lt=Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999|WD=Q33082999|1=ukiyo-e artist}} who was active in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka|1=UNKNOWN}} in the middle and late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. The {{ill|lt=Thirty-six Poems|Q671039|WD=Q671039|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}43 ({{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{ill|lt=Nakagaku|Q460152|WD=Q460152|1=Nakatsukasa}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{ill|lt=Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198|WD=Q335198|1=Kantarō Suzuki}} - 42nd {{ill|lt=Prime Minister|Q274948|WD=Q274948|1=Prime Minister of Japan}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{ill|lt=February 26 Incident|Q477958|WD=Q477958|1=February 26 incident}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]]

Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyayamadai|Q60997456|WD=Q60997456|1=Miyayamadai}}. It is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, which is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The old {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} is {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} (around {{ill|lt=530|Q30894|WD=Q30894|1=530}}). Until the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The main shrine is a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} building and is designated as a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}38), with the development plan of {{ill|lt=Senboku New Town|Q7450095|WD=Q7450095|1=Senboku New Town}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{ill|lt=Himekami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Tajhayahime no Mikoto|1=Himegami}} * Combined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Sujinson|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{ill|lt=Important cultural property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{ill|lt=1539|Q6479|WD=Q6479|1=1539}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{ill|lt=1543|Q6523|WD=Q6523|1=1543}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{ill|lt=toad-mata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{ill|lt=hand clip|Q124660898|WD=Q124660898|1=tabasami}} of the facing prayer is {{ill|lt=openwork|Q17055244|WD=Q17055244|1=Sukashibori}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{ill|lt=1541|Q6516|WD=Q6516|1=1541}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}41) from {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q646275|WD=Q646275|1=Senboku District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869|WD=Q11400869|1=Kitaniwa}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshirosu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Taneko Ota|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|1=Ōtataneko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Omi Takamikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{ill|lt=Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|WD=Q11368908|lt=Kuse Village|1=Kuze}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=surname record|1=UNKNOWN}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nao Ikebe|1=UNKNOWN}} is the same ancestor as {{ill|lt=Sakagami clan|Q10928477|WD=Q10928477|lt=Sakagami Ōsukune|1=Sakanoue clan}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{ill|lt=Achi Messhi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitototo Toto no Kami|1=Hitokotonushi}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko no Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Inari God|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Spirit Life|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman God|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Honda Bessumei|1=Hachiman}}. {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Shrine office * Ritual hall * Sanshuden * Shinkan * Suitengu Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property === * Main shrine attached: ridge plate (1 piece) === Festival === * Saidan Festival ** {{ill|lt=January 1st|Q2150|WD=Q2150|1=January 1}} 8am * Kobudo dedication ** {{ill|lt=January 3rd|Q2156|WD=Q2156|1=January 3}} 10am * Fukuishi Shrine ritual ** {{ill|lt=January 6th|Q2221|WD=Q2221|1=January 6}} 2pm * Tondoyaki ** Third Sunday in January from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. * Setsubun Festival ** {{ill|lt=February 3rd|Q2315|WD=Q2315|1=February 3}} 10am * Fire-burning ritual to ward off evil spirits **February 3rd from 7pm * Inari Festival ** Last Sunday in March 10am * Summer exorcism ceremony ** {{ill|lt=June 30th|Q2657|WD=Q2657|1=June 30}} 4pm * Benten Festival **Last Sunday in August 6pm * Reitaisai ** Sunday 9am near {{ill|lt=October 5th|Q2930|WD=Q2930|1=October 5}} ** Crowded with parishioners holding {{ill|lt=danjiri|Q11271630|WD=Q11271630|1=danjiri}} and mikoshi danjiri shrine entrances. * Shichigosan pilgrimage ** {{ill|lt=November 15th|Q2982|WD=Q2982|1=November 15}} * New year's purification ceremony ** {{ill|lt=December 31st|Q2912|WD=Q2912|1=December 31}} * Monthly festival ** 6am on the 1st of every month === Local information === * {{ill|lt=Nankai Senboku Line|Q2758012|WD=Q2758012|1=Semboku Line}} From {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izumigaoka Station|1=UNKNOWN}}, take the {{ill|lt=Nankai Bus|Q11408154|WD=Q11408154|1=Nankai Bus}} "Miyayamadai-bound", "Sakai-Higashi bound", or "(via Senboku Route 2) bound for Tsukuno Station" and get off at "Miyayamadai 2-chome". === Footnotes === === References === * Edited by {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education|Q7105593|WD=Q7105593|1=Osaka Prefectural Board of Education}} “Important Cultural Property Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall Repair Report” 1956 * Osaka Prefecture History Walk Editorial Committee, New Edition Osaka Prefecture History Walk Part 2, Yamakawa Publishing, 1990, p. 181 * "Sakai/Senshu" No. 13, Sakai Senshu Publishing, 2002, pp. 79-81 * “Sakai History Reader” KADOKAWA, 2015, p. 117 === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Yu Guangetsu|Q11621791|WD=Q11621791|1=Shitomi Kangetsu}} - {{ill|lt=Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999|WD=Q33082999|1=ukiyo-e artist}} who was active in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka|1=UNKNOWN}} in the middle and late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. The {{ill|lt=Thirty-six Poems|Q671039|WD=Q671039|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}43 ({{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{ill|lt=Nakagaku|Q460152|WD=Q460152|1=Nakatsukasa}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{ill|lt=Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198|WD=Q335198|1=Kantarō Suzuki}} - 42nd {{ill|lt=Prime Minister|Q274948|WD=Q274948|1=Prime Minister of Japan}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{ill|lt=February 26 Incident|Q477958|WD=Q477958|1=February 26 incident}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]]

Hime Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} {{osm box|w|368230196}} [[File:Tajihayahime-jinja Hall of Worship Front shrine of Tajihayahime-jinja 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Shrine of Worship]] [[File:Tajisuhime Shrine Main Hall and Kamota Shrine in Minami Ward, Sakai City 2012.12.14 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|200px|Main Hall (Important Cultural Property)]]'''Tajhayahime Shrine'' (Tajihayahime Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai City|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Minami Ward (Sakai City)|Q1041996|WD=Q1041996|lt=Minami Ward|1=Minami-ku, Sakai}} {{ill|lt=Shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyayamadai|Q60997456|WD=Q60997456|1=Miyayamadai}}. It is the {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}} of {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, which is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. The old {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} is {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called ``Arayama Shrine'' (Kozen no Miya), and the area around the precincts is crowded with people as {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}}. === History === {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}{{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}} It is one of the 24 Engishiki naisha shrines, and is said to have been built in the era of {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} (around {{ill|lt=530|Q30894|WD=Q30894|1=530}}). Until the first year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, it was {{ill|lt=Jinguji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}, which existed alongside Sofukuji Temple, but when {{ill|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, it became only a shrine. Later it was listed as {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The main shrine is a {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} building and is designated as a national {{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}. There are 13 subsidiary shrines within the precincts: Sakagami Shrine (Shikiuchi), Kamota Shrine (Shikinai), Daijinja, Sumiyoshi, Amaterasu, Hachiman, Kasuga, Kumano, Hakusan, Benten, Inari, Fukuishi, and Suitengu, which are collectively known as Arayama Shrine. In {{ill|lt=1963|Q2755|WD=Q2755|1=1963}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}38), with the development plan of {{ill|lt=Senboku New Town|Q7450095|WD=Q7450095|1=Senboku New Town}}, most of the company-owned land was sold and houses and {{ill|lt=Arayama Park|Q11618489|WD=Q11618489|1=Kozen koen}} were built. As a result, our company is undergoing maintenance of its precincts and major renovations of its worship hall, gathering hall, etc. === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{ill|lt=Himekami|Q22070227|WD=Q22070227|lt=Tajhayahime no Mikoto|1=Himegami}} * Combined deities - {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|WD=Q272993|lt=Sujinson|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} === Precincts === * Main shrine ({{ill|lt=Important cultural property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - {{jalink|astronomy of {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} (era name)|Q1192853|Tenbun}} Reconstruction between 8 years ({{ill|lt=1539|Q6479|WD=Q6479|1=1539}}) and 12 years of Tenbun ({{ill|lt=1543|Q6523|WD=Q6523|1=1543}}). The style is Sangensha irimoya-zukuri, with a houndstooth gable in front, a large bower, and an eaves-shaped gable. The {{ill|lt=toad-mata|Q123117911|WD=Q123117911|1=kaerumata}} in the upper center of the prayer pillar has dragons, clouds, and waves, and the left and right toad-mata have peonies and Chinese lions on the obverse, clouds and jewels on the back, and inside. The front center of the Honagoshi is carved with paulownia, the right one is carved with a horse mackerel, the left one is carved with sasanqua flowers, pine trees, and coins, and the north and south sides are carved with carp, pine, waterfalls, and clouds. The {{ill|lt=hand clip|Q124660898|WD=Q124660898|1=tabasami}} of the facing prayer is {{ill|lt=openwork|Q17055244|WD=Q17055244|1=Sukashibori}}, and there are two on the left and right; the right side has a Basho and mantis carved on the right side, a lotus on the water on the left side, seaweed and shellfish on the right side, and irises on the left side. The wooden nose of Mukaihai has a strange and unusual shape resembling the head of an animal. This is said to be the only sculpture in the country of Basho with a mantis. During the dismantling and repair work, ink calligraphy from the 8th year of Tenbun (1539) and 10th year ({{ill|lt=1541|Q6516|WD=Q6516|1=1541}}) was discovered; nk|1819|Q6967}})) says that it was rebuilt in February of the 12th year of Tenbun (1543), which makes it clear that it was built over a period of 12 years from the 8th year of the Tenbun era (1543).* Kamota Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} is on the right hand side when facing the main shrine. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}41) from {{ill|lt=Senboku District|Q646275|WD=Q646275|1=Senboku District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kitakami Kamimura|Q11400869|WD=Q11400869|1=Kitaniwa}} Oaza Daihei-ji Temple Kamota. {{ill|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamobetsu Rai no Mikoto|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Mikami|Q1114013|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi no Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshirosu no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{jali nk|Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|Takeuchi Sukune no Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Taneko Ota|Q55536798|WD=Q55536798|1=Ōtataneko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Omi Takamikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, etc., but the basis for each is unknown.* Sakagami Shrine - Enshrined deity: unknown. Shikinai-sha is on the left as you face the main shrine. In {{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}} (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from Senboku County {{ill|lt=Kuse Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11368908|WD=Q11368908|lt=Kuse Village|1=Kuze}} Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. According to the ``{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=surname record|1=UNKNOWN}}'' and the various clans of Izumi Province, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nao Ikebe|1=UNKNOWN}} is the same ancestor as {{ill|lt=Sakagami clan|Q10928477|WD=Q10928477|lt=Sakagami Ōsukune|1=Sakanoue clan}} and a descendant of King Achi. Furthermore, in the section of ``Izumi Province Jinmyocho'' {{ill|lt=Otori County|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, the three shrines of Ikebe Hinohara Shrine, Ikebe Shrine Shrine, and Ikebe Shrine, which are listed between Kusakabe Otoshi Shrine, Jindai Shrine, Sakanoue Shrine, and others, are thought to be shrines located in Hachita-go or Kusakabe-go, based on the order in which they are listed. Based on these facts, it is speculated that the Sakagami Shrine located in Hirai, Hachita-go was where the Ikebe clan or the Sakagami clan enshrined their ancestor {{ill|lt=Achi Messhi|Q11657596|WD=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} (Achi King). ``Sakai City History Sequel'' Volume 1 (Part 1, Ancient times, p. 179) states that there is a strong possibility that the Ikebe Nao family lived in Otori County in ancient times. * Kuzu Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Hitototo no Mushi|Q10864079|WD=Q10864079|lt=Hitototo Toto no Kami|1=Hitokotonushi}}. It is enshrined together from Nakagaki, Oaza Hirai, Kuze Village. * Kinmine Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kanayamahikojin|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko no Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}・Kanayamahime no Mikoto. It is enshrined together from Kuze Village Oaza Hirai Aza Minamigakigai. * Amaterasusha * Big shrine * Sumiyoshisha * Hall of worship * Hakusansha * Kumanosha * Kasugasha* Bentensha (Itsukushima Shrine) - Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}. It was enshrined in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40) from Oaza Obaji, Itsukushima, Kitakami-mura, Senboku-gun. * {{ill|lt=Ise Jingu|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} Far place of worship * Statue of Yoshida Minzo Chief Priest * Kanadaka Inari Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Inari God|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari Spirit Life|1=Inari Ōkami}} * Side gate * Fukuishi Shrine - Enshrined deity: Fukuishi Okami * Old Daishido * Hachiman Shrine - Enshrined Deity: {{ill|lt=Hachiman God|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Honda Bessumei|1=Hachiman}}. {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|WD=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), it was enshrined at Fushioyama, Oaza Fushio, Kuze Village, Senboku District, and in 1910 (Meiji 43), it was enshrined from the top of Oaza Wada, Yama, Kuze Village, Senboku District. * Guangetsu|Q11621791|WD=Q11621791|1=Shitomi Kangetsu}} - {{ill|lt=Ukiyo-e artist|Q33082999|WD=Q33082999|1=ukiyo-e artist}} who was active in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Osaka|1=UNKNOWN}} in the middle and late {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}. The {{ill|lt=Thirty-six Poems|Q671039|WD=Q671039|1=Thirty-Six Immortals of Poetry}} plaque was moved from Sakagami Shrine in Hirai in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}43 ({{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|WD=Q2075|1=1910}}). There were originally 36 pages, but only half remain today. Among these, the ink on the back of the {{ill|lt=Nakagaku|Q460152|WD=Q460152|1=Nakatsukasa}} says ``Artist: Mr. Hokyashi Sekiyue-sho'', indicating that he had obtained the Hokyo rank before this. * {{ill|lt=Kantaro Suzuki|Q335198|WD=Q335198|1=Kantarō Suzuki}} - 42nd {{ill|lt=Prime Minister|Q274948|WD=Q274948|1=Prime Minister of Japan}}. Although he left his hometown at a young age, he had a deep reverence for Tajihayahime Shrine and even visited it when he was a lieutenant colonel. A large plaque with the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki written on it, ``Respectful God, Worshipful Ancestor,'' still remains, and is currently hung in the parishioners gathering hall, and fans with the same inscription were reproduced and divided among the parishioners. When the main hall of the Inari Shrine, a shrine within the shrine precincts, was renovated and a new Tamagaki constructed in 1921, the Tamagaki stone dedicated in the name of Navy Vice Admiral Kantaro Suzuki remains. Also, when he was seriously injured in the {{ill|lt=February 26 Incident|Q477958|WD=Q477958|1=February 26 incident}}, Tajisahime no Mikoto stood at his bedside and saved his life, and in January of the following year, he and his wife Taka visited the shrine to commemorate his return. After the war, when the idea of ​​building Suzuki Shrine came up, it was discussed that the grounds of Tajiwa Hime Shrine would be suitable. === External link === * [http://tajihayahime.com/ Tajihayahime Shrine top page] (official website) {{commons|Category:Tajihayahime-jinja}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tajihayahime Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:堺市南区の歴史]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Sakai City]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:堺市南区の建築物]] [[Category:泉北ニュータウン]]
